conditional_states.condition: Paginated or infinite-scroll table where only some rows are loaded.
Data can change server-side and is cached or polled rather than live.
Large result sets that may take noticeable time to fetch or sort.
Rows or bulk actions are not actionable in the current context.
Table backed by data that should remain readable without connectivity.
User may see the table but cannot access certain rows or row actions.
Wide tables must remain navigable under 200% zoom or large fonts.
Row selection and actions must work without hover on coarse-pointer devices.

rationale: Tables present many rows and selectable actions, so per-row hover, keyboard navigation between cells/rows, and a visible selected state for bulk operations are required. A first-class empty state (no rows yet vs no matches) and a distinct error state prevent users from mistaking a failed fetch for genuinely empty data.
evidence: WCAG 2.2 2.1.1 (Keyboard) requires full keyboard operability of interactive grids.
ARIA grid pattern (WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices) defines roving-focus keyboard navigation for data tables.
NN/g empty-state guidance: distinguish 'no data yet', 'no results for filter', and 'failed to load'.
